paper_title: Surface Model and Surface Fluxes in GCM

methods.description: This method involves calculating temperatures in the surface and sub-surface layers of a land model coupled with a GCM. It accounts for various heat fluxes and uses a multi-layer approach to model heat conduction.

steps.description: The atmospheric component of the GCM is coupled to a land model that calculates temperatures in the surface and sub-surface.
steps.searchable_summary: couple atmospheric GCM with land model to calculate surface and sub-surface temperatures

steps.description: Fluxes of sensible and latent heat, as well as radiative fluxes (including emitted thermal flux), affect the top-most surface layer.
steps.searchable_summary: apply heat fluxes to top-most surface layer

steps.description: Conduction transports heat between soil layers. We use 15 layers of variable thickness, and assume a constant thermal conductivity and volumetric heat capacity.
steps.searchable_summary: model heat conduction between 15 soil layers

methods.description: This method tracks the presence of surface liquid using a 'bucket' model and ensures the conservation of methane in the system.

steps.description: The surface model tracks the presence of surface liquid with a 'bucket' model: Liquid from precipitation is allowed to accumulate, and is then available to evaporate.
steps.searchable_summary: implement bucket model to track surface liquid accumulation and evaporation

steps.description: Below a threshold amount of liquid, a linearly decreasing availability factor roughly parameterizes infiltration and sub-grid scale ponding, limiting evaporation.
steps.searchable_summary: apply availability factor to limit evaporation based on liquid threshold

steps.description: The total amount of methane in the system (surface liquid and atmospheric moisture) is conserved.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure conservation of methane in surface liquid and atmospheric moisture

methods.description: This method computes surface fluxes of momentum, heat, and vapor using bulk aerodynamic formulae, incorporating specific drag coefficients and roughness parameters.

steps.description: Surface fluxes of momentum, heat, and vapor are computed using bulk aerodynamic formulae, with drag coefficients from Monin-Obukhov similarity theory.
steps.searchable_summary: compute surface fluxes using bulk aerodynamic formulae and Monin-Obukhov theory

steps.description: The roughness length and gustiness parameters in this module are set to 0.5 cm and 0.1 m s −1, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: set roughness length and gustiness parameters
